title: Battle of T'olan
content:
{{Top|fca|leg}} {{Battle |prev= |conc= |next= |image= |name=Battle of T'olan |conflict=[[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'' |date=c. [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] lasted from [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. The battle of [[T'olan]] was placed as "early in the wars" by ''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'', which has been interpreted in this article as sometime around the first [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. |place=[[T'olan]] |result= |side1=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |side2= |side3= |side4= |commanders1= |commanders2= |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] **[[Trever Flume's mother]]† |forces2= |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=[[Trever Flume]]'s [[Parent/Legends|mother]] |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|My mother was a captain in the Grand Army of the Republic. She died in the battle of T'olan, in the Wuun system…''"
"''I know it. That was a terrible battle.|Trever Flume and Obi-Wan Kenobi|}} The '''Battle of T'olan''' took place early in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], sometime around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], when the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] fought at [[T'olan]], a location in the [[Wuun system]]. During the fierce conflict, a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]], the [[Trever Flume's mother|mother]] of a [[Gender/Legends|boy]] named [[Trever Flume]], was [[Death/Legends|killed]]. ==Prelude== The [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] began in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] when a number of [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], allied as the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], seceded from the dominant [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government, the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' In order to fight with the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]], a [[Trever Flume's mother|woman]] left [[Gender/Legends|her]] family on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Bellassa/Legends|Bellassa]]. Just before she departed, she told her son, [[Trever Flume]], that he could not join her in the fight and promised to return to him safely. ==The battle== Early in the war, the Republic fought at a location known as [[T'olan]] in the [[Wuun system]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Wuun system|T'olan system]] The battle was [[Blood/Legends|bloody]], and Trever Flume's mother [[Death/Legends|died]] there, fighting as a [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|You're not going without me.''"
"''It's a two-person ship. There's no room. I'll be back—''"
"''No! Don't say that. Just… don't. I can help.|Trever Flume persuades [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Ferus Olin]] to take him to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]|''[[The Last of the Jedi: Underworld]]''}} The Clone Wars concluded with the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the Republic's successor, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. By [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places ''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'' in "c. [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]]." the Empire had subjugated the planet of Bellassa and killed [[Trever Flume's father]] and [[Tike Flume|brother]] in a bombing. Orphaned by this, Trever Flume spiraled into an angry, confused depression''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' from which he emerged as a member of the Bellassan [[Black market/Legends|black market]], haunted by the deaths of each family member. Because of what his mother had said before she left, and his father and brother saying much the same thing before their deaths, he became deeply nervous when people he cared about promised to return from danger without allowing him to come. [[Standard month/Legends|Months]] after being orphaned, Trevor Flume explained his situation to the former [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] had passed since the battle of T'olan, yet Kenobi recalled its deadly reputation when Flume mentioned his mother's death. ==Behind the scenes== The battle of T'olan was first mentioned in the young reader novel ''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'' ([[2005]]) by author [[Jude Watson]]. The name of the battle was not capitalized in the novel. In ''The Desperate Mission'', Trever Flume said that his mother had fought in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], but this could not have been the case. ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' ([[2012]]) states that only [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] served in the Grand Army.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' Those [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] were all men''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' identifies the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] as an "[[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Cloning/Legends|identical]] [[Gender/Legends|men]]." and nigh''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]'' stars the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] [[X1]] and [[X2]], whose [[Falon Grey|genetic template]] was not [[Jango Fett]]. exclusively sourced from the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. On the other hand, Jedi Generals and [[Jedi Commander/Legends|Jedi Commanders]] were considered part of the Grand Army and were not clones, but they significantly outranked the [[Clone Captain/Legends|captains]], who were clones. Nonetheless, there were non-clone branches of the Military fighting for the Republic, such as the [[Planetary Security Forces]] or the [[Judicial/Legends|Judicials]]. Another [[Star Wars: The Last of the Jedi|''The Last of the Jedi'']] book, [[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo|''Death on Naboo'']] ([[2006]]), said that the Galactic Empire had killed Trever Flume's mother while she was fighting against them,''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'' but the Galactic Empire had not yet been founded when she died.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the [[Declaration of a New Order|founding]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], immediately before the [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|end]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. According to ''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'', [[Trever Flume's mother]] [[Death/Legends|died]] "early in the wars." Trever also remembered his mother dying on Bellassa, rather than at T'olan, in ''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'' (2006).''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'' These books conflated her with her partner and other son, whom ''The Desperate Mission'' established as having died at Imperial hands on Bellassa, but ''A Tangled Web'' also explicitly mentioned the deaths of those two as independent of hers. ==Appearances== *''[[The Last of the Jedi: The Desperate Mission]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Underworld]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Death on Naboo]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Reckoning]]'' {{Imo}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|I||Flume, Trever}} {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von T'olan }} {{DEFAULTSORT:T'olan, Battle of}} [[Category:Battles in the Tiala sector]] [[Category:Battles of the Clone Wars]]
